items.title: Democrats press CFTC, ethics watchdog on prediction market insider trading
items.summary: Prediction markets are facing increasing scrutiny over allegations of insider trading, prompting Kalshi and Polymarket to plan guardrails to curb potential incidents.

items.title: Bitcoin demand falters as 'real' interest rates surge
items.summary: Rising U.S. real yields, especially on 10-year TIPS, pose a headwind to zero-yielding risk assets like bitcoin.

items.title: Crypto exchange KuCoin agrees to $500K settlement, ending CFTC case
items.summary: KuCoin’s parent company will pay $500,000 to resolve CFTC charges, marking a fraction of the $300 million it previously agreed to pay the DOJ over similar charges.

items.title: Quantum computers need fewer qubits to crack crypto than thought: Google
items.summary: Google has updated its estimates of the quantum computing resources needed to break elliptic curve cryptography.

items.title: Dubai sets formal rules for crypto exchange-traded derivatives
items.summary: Dubai’s VARA has set formal rules for crypto exchange-traded derivatives, allowing retail access under suitability checks, margin controls and leverage limits.

items.title: Bitcoin holds $67,500 as Trump signals he may end Iran war with Hormuz still shut
items.summary: Equity futures rallied and oil erased gains on the report, but the S&P 500 is on its longest losing streak since 2022 and MSCI Asia Pacific is heading for its worst month since 2008.

items.title: New US Rule Seeks to Open $8T Retirement Market to Crypto
items.summary: The safe harbor proposal would allow 401(k) managers to offer crypto-linked funds with stronger legal protections.

items.title: Bitmine scoops $147M of ETH in its biggest week of buying this year

items.title: Maryland man charged in $50 million Uranium Finance hack after U.S. seized $31 million in crypto

items.summary: Historical data shows Bitcoin bear markets deepening when oil prices rally to record highs. Will Monday’s $105 WTI price lead to a BTC crash?

items.title: Peter Brandt, Polymarket traders don’t see new Bitcoin highs this year
items.summary: Polymarket pundits are giving just a 15% chance that Bitcoin will reclaim $120,000 in 2026, while veteran trader Peter Brandt said he doesn't expect a new high until Q2 2027.
